1	---
2	ddx:
3	  id: plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout
4	  depends_on:
5	    - FEAT-011
6	    - FEAT-015
7	    - FEAT-018
8	---
9	# DDx Skill Package Layout
10	
11	Date: 2026-05-13
12	Status: Draft
13	
14	## Summary
15	
16	The DDx skill tree should be content owned by the default `ddx` plugin package,
17	not a parallel bootstrap tree with checked-in mirrors. `ddx init`, `ddx update`,
18	and `ddx plugin install ddx` should all install the same package layout through
19	the same package installer.
20	
21	Development installs may use symlink overlays for live editing, but those
22	overlays are project-local. DDx must not restore home-directory skill installs;
23	the only expected home binary is `~/.local/bin/ddx`.
24	
25	## Current Problems
26	
27	- `skills/ddx/` is the editable source, but `cli/internal/skills/ddx/`,
28	  `.agents/skills/ddx/`, `.claude/skills/ddx/`, and `.ddx/skills/ddx/` are
29	  full copied mirrors.
30	- `cli/Makefile copy-skills` syncs the top-level skill tree into embedded and
31	  project-local copies, so editing behavior depends on remembering which mirror
32	  is canonical.
33	- `ddx init` installs shipped skills through a direct bootstrap path using
34	  `skills.SkillFiles`, then separately installs the default `ddx` plugin
35	  package.
36	- `ddx update` refreshes shipped skills through the same direct embedded-skill
37	  path rather than the package installer.
38	- `ddx plugin install ddx --local .` is unsafe in the current repo layout
39	  because local install prefers `<pluginRoot>/.agents/skills` before
40	  `<pluginRoot>/skills`; pointed at the repo root, that can target the
41	  destination skill tree instead of the intended source.
42	
43	## Target Layout
44	
45	The default package owns the DDx skill:
46	
47	```text
48	library/
49	  package.yaml
50	  skills/
51	    ddx/
52	      SKILL.md
53	      reference/
54	      bead-lifecycle/
55	      ...
56	```
57	
58	The checked-in editable source is `library/skills/ddx/`.
59	
60	The release binary embeds the default package content, not a special skill-only
61	tree:
62	
63	```text
64	library/ -> cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/
65	```
66	
67	The project-local discovered skill paths are install outputs:
68	
69	```text
70	.agents/skills/ddx  -> installed by package installer
71	.claude/skills/ddx  -> installed by package installer
72	```
73	
74	For registry installs, these outputs are real files. For local development
75	overlays, they may be symlinks to the source checkout.
76	
77	## Manifest Contract
78	
79	`library/package.yaml` declares package-local skill sources:
80	
81	```yaml
82	install:
83	  root:
84	    source: .
85	    target: .ddx/plugins/ddx
86	  skills:
87	    - source: skills/
88	      target: .agents/skills/
89	    - source: skills/
90	      target: .claude/skills/
91	```
92	
93	The installer must honor `install.skills[*].source` relative to the package
94	root. Skill discovery must not hard-code `.agents/skills` as the primary source
95	when the manifest explicitly says `skills/`.
96	
97	Directory-to-directory skill mappings install the child skill directories under
98	the target. The example above installs `skills/ddx` to `.agents/skills/ddx` and
99	`.claude/skills/ddx`; it does not replace `.agents/skills` or `.claude/skills`
100	as whole directories. This keeps unrelated project skills visible.
101	
102	## Installer Contract
103	
104	Split the package installer into source-agnostic operations:
105	
106	```go
107	InstallPackageFromRemote(pkg, projectRoot)
108	InstallPackageFromDir(pkg, sourceDir, projectRoot)
109	InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, sourceFS, projectRoot)
110	```
111	
112	All three paths must share the same core install implementation:
113	
114	1. load and validate `package.yaml` when present;
115	2. apply `install.root.source -> install.root.target`;
116	3. apply every `install.skills[*].source -> install.skills[*].target`;
117	4. apply scripts/executables with the existing project-scope rules;
118	5. return one `InstalledEntry` shape.
119	
120	Remote and embedded installs write real files. Local installs create developer
121	overlays: `.ddx/plugins/<name>` and plugin-owned skill outputs are symlinks to
122	the local checkout. Local overlays do not mutate recorded plugin pins and do not
123	auto-commit.
124	
125	## Init And Update Contract
126	
127	`ddx init` installs the default DDx package through the embedded package
128	installer. It no longer calls a separate embedded skill installer and no longer
129	maintains `.ddx/skills/ddx` as a bootstrap-only mirror.
130	
131	`ddx update` / the forward replacement for shipped-content refresh uses the
132	same package installer path as `ddx init`.
133	
134	Remove these direct bootstrap mechanisms after replacement tests exist:
135	
136	- `skills.Install(skills.SkillFiles, ...)` calls from `ddx init` and update;
137	- `registerBootstrapDDxSkills`;
138	- `cli/internal/skills/ddx` as a distinct embedded source;
139	- `skills/ddx` as the top-level canonical source;
140	- checked-in `.agents/skills/ddx`, `.claude/skills/ddx`, and `.ddx/skills/ddx`
141	  mirrors.
142	
143	## Development Workflow
144	
145	The repo development workflow becomes:
146	
147	```bash
148	ddx plugin install ddx --local library --force
149	```
150	
151	After that command, edits to `library/skills/ddx/` are live through the
152	project-local harness discovery paths.
153	
154	The command must be safe when run from the DDx repo. If someone passes the repo
155	root instead of `library`, the installer must either resolve to the manifest's
156	declared package root or fail with a clear error; it must not self-link
157	`.agents/skills/ddx`.
158	
159	## Migration Sequence
160	
161	1. Add embedded default-plugin package support for `library/`.
162	2. Teach the installer to honor `install.skills[*].source` for remote,
163	   embedded, and local installs.
164	3. Change `ddx init` to install `ddx` through the embedded package installer.
165	4. Change shipped-content refresh/update to use the same package installer.
166	5. Move `skills/ddx/` to `library/skills/ddx/`.
167	6. Replace `copy-skills` with a default-package embed sync:
168	   `library/ -> cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/`.
169	7. Remove the checked-in skill mirrors after tests prove install outputs are
170	   produced by init/local install.
171	8. Update FEAT-011, FEAT-015, SD-011, and any command help or AGENTS blocks that
172	   describe copied bootstrap skill mirrors.
173	
174	## Required Tests
175	
176	- `TestInitInstallsDDxPluginPackage`: `ddx init` creates `.ddx/plugins/ddx`,
177	  `.agents/skills/ddx`, and `.claude/skills/ddx` through the package installer.
178	- `TestPluginInstallLocalDDxLibrarySymlinksSkills`: `ddx plugin install ddx
179	  --local library --force` creates project-local symlinks to
180	  `library/skills/ddx`.
181	- `TestPluginInstallHonorsSkillSource`: a fixture with `install.skills.source:
182	  skills/` installs skills from `skills/` and does not require
183	  `.agents/skills` inside the source package.
184	- `TestPluginInstallPreservesUnrelatedProjectSkills`: installing the `ddx`
185	  package does not replace `.agents/skills` or `.claude/skills` when other
186	  skills already exist there.
187	- `TestInitWorksOfflineWithEmbeddedDefaultPlugin`: no network is needed for
188	  `ddx init` to install the default package and skill.
189	- `TestRepoRootLocalInstallDoesNotSelfLink`: passing the DDx repo root either
190	  resolves to the package root safely or fails before modifying skill outputs.
191	- `ddx skills check library/skills/ddx .agents/skills/ddx .claude/skills/ddx`
192	  passes after local install.
193	- `make skill-schema` validates the package-owned skill source and embedded
194	  default-package copy.
195	
196	## Non-Scope
197	
198	- Do not restore home-directory skill installs.
199	- Do not change the public plugin CLI names beyond the separate plugin CLI
200	  rationalization plan.
201	- Do not remove the embedded package copy until offline `ddx init` is green.
202

1	// Package defaultplugin holds the embedded default `ddx` plugin package.
2	//
3	// The embedded `library/` tree is rooted at the package layout consumed by
4	// the registry installer. It lets `ddx init` install the default plugin
5	// offline through the same code path as a remote install. See
6	// docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout.md.
7	package defaultplugin
8	
9	import (
10		"embed"
11		"io/fs"
12	)
13	
14	//go:embed all:library
15	var embedded embed.FS
16	
17	// FS returns a filesystem rooted at the embedded default-plugin package
18	// (the equivalent of the on-disk `library/` directory). The returned fs.FS
19	// is suitable for passing to registry.InstallPackageFromFS.
20	func FS() fs.FS {
21		sub, err := fs.Sub(embedded, "library")
22		if err != nil {
23			// embed roots are verified at compile time; a sub failure means the
24			// embedded tree shape changed under us.
25			panic(err)
26		}
27		return sub
28	}
29

1	name: ddx
2	version: 0.4.7
3	description: DDx default library — prompts, personas, MCP server configs, environment templates, and skills
4	type: plugin
5	source: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx
6	api_version: "1"
7	keywords:
8	  - library
9	  - prompts
10	  - personas
11	  - mcp
12	  - default
13	  - skills
14	install:
15	  root:
16	    source: .
17	    target: .ddx/plugins/ddx
18	  skills:
19	    - source: skills/
20	      target: .agents/skills/
21	    - source: skills/
22	      target: .claude/skills/
23

1	package registry
2	
3	import (
4		"archive/tar"
5		"bytes"
6		"compress/gzip"
7		"net/http"
8		"net/http/httptest"
9		"os"
10		"path/filepath"
11		"sort"
12		"strings"
13		"testing"
14		"testing/fstest"
15	
16		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/registry/defaultplugin"
17		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
18		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
19	)
20	
21	func TestInstallPackageHonorsSkillSource(t *testing.T) {
22		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
23		server := newPackageArchiveServer(t, samplePackageTarball(t))
24		defer server.Close()
25	
26		pkg := sampleInstallPackage(server.URL)
27		entry, err := InstallPackage(pkg, projectRoot)
28		require.NoError(t, err)
29	
30		for _, rel := range []string{
31			filepath.Join(".agents", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
32			filepath.Join(".claude", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
33		} {
34			path := filepath.Join(projectRoot, rel)
35			data, readErr := os.ReadFile(path)
36			require.NoError(t, readErr)
37			assert.Contains(t, string(data), "ddx")
38		}
39	
40		for _, rel := range []string{
41			filepath.Join(".agents", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
42			filepath.Join(".claude", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
43		} {
44			assert.Contains(t, entry.Files, rel)
45		}
46	}
47	
48	func TestInstallPackagePreservesUnrelatedProjectSkills(t *testing.T) {
49		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
50		server := newPackageArchiveServer(t, samplePackageTarball(t))
51		defer server.Close()
52	
53		for _, rel := range []string{
54			filepath.Join(".agents", "skills", "existing-skill", "SKILL.md"),
55			filepath.Join(".claude", "skills", "existing-skill", "SKILL.md"),
56		} {
57			path := filepath.Join(projectRoot, rel)
58			require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755))
59			require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(path, []byte("keep me"), 0o644))
60		}
61	
62		_, err := InstallPackage(sampleInstallPackage(server.URL), projectRoot)
63		require.NoError(t, err)
64	
65		for _, rel := range []string{
66			filepath.Join(".agents", "skills", "existing-skill", "SKILL.md"),
67			filepath.Join(".claude", "skills", "existing-skill", "SKILL.md"),
68		} {
69			path := filepath.Join(projectRoot, rel)
70			data, readErr := os.ReadFile(path)
71			require.NoError(t, readErr)
72			assert.Equal(t, "keep me", string(data))
73		}
74	}
75	
76	func TestInstallPackageRecordsManifestSkillTargets(t *testing.T) {
77		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
78		server := newPackageArchiveServer(t, samplePackageTarball(t))
79		defer server.Close()
80	
81		entry, err := InstallPackage(sampleInstallPackage(server.URL), projectRoot)
82		require.NoError(t, err)
83	
84		for _, rel := range []string{
85			filepath.Join(".agents", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
86			filepath.Join(".claude", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
87		} {
88			assert.Contains(t, entry.Files, rel)
89		}
90	}
91	
92	func sampleInstallPackage(source string) *Package {
93		return &Package{
94			Name:    "sample-plugin",
95			Version: "1.0.0",
96			Type:    PackageTypePlugin,
97			Source:  source,
98			Install: PackageInstall{
99				Root: &InstallMapping{
100					Source: ".",
101					Target: ".ddx/plugins/sample-plugin",
102				},
103				Skills: []InstallMapping{
104					{Source: "skills/", Target: ".agents/skills/"},
105					{Source: "skills/", Target: ".claude/skills/"},
106				},
107			},
108		}
109	}
110	
111	func samplePackageTarball(t *testing.T) []byte {
112		t.Helper()
113	
114		var buf bytes.Buffer
115		gz := gzip.NewWriter(&buf)
116		tw := tar.NewWriter(gz)
117	
118		topDir := "sample-plugin-1.0.0"
119		writeTarDir(t, tw, topDir)
120		writeTarFile(t, tw, filepath.Join(topDir, "package.yaml"), []byte(`name: sample-plugin
121	version: 1.0.0
122	description: Sample package
123	type: plugin
124	source: https://example.com/sample-plugin
125	api_version: 1
126	install:
127	  root:
128	    source: .
129	    target: .ddx/plugins/sample-plugin
130	  skills:
131	    - source: skills/
132	      target: .agents/skills/
133	    - source: skills/
134	      target: .claude/skills/
135	`))
136		writeTarDir(t, tw, filepath.Join(topDir, "skills"))
137		writeTarDir(t, tw, filepath.Join(topDir, "skills", "ddx"))
138		writeTarFile(t, tw, filepath.Join(topDir, "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"), []byte("---\nname: ddx\ndescription: sample\n---\n# ddx\n"))
139	
140		require.NoError(t, tw.Close())
141		require.NoError(t, gz.Close())
142		return buf.Bytes()
143	}
144	
145	func newPackageArchiveServer(t *testing.T, payload []byte) *httptest.Server {
146		t.Helper()
147	
148		return httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
149			if !strings.HasSuffix(r.URL.Path, "/archive/refs/tags/v1.0.0.tar.gz") {
150				t.Errorf("unexpected archive path: %s", r.URL.Path)
151			}
152			w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/gzip")
153			_, _ = w.Write(payload)
154		}))
155	}
156	
157	func writeTarDir(t *testing.T, tw *tar.Writer, name string) {
158		t.Helper()
159	
160		require.NoError(t, tw.WriteHeader(&tar.Header{
161			Name:     filepath.ToSlash(name) + "/",
162			Mode:     0o755,
163			Typeflag: tar.TypeDir,
164		}))
165	}
166	
167	func writeTarFile(t *testing.T, tw *tar.Writer, name string, body []byte) {
168		t.Helper()
169	
170		require.NoError(t, tw.WriteHeader(&tar.Header{
171			Name:     filepath.ToSlash(name),
172			Mode:     0o644,
173			Size:     int64(len(body)),
174			Typeflag: tar.TypeReg,
175		}))
176		_, err := tw.Write(body)
177		require.NoError(t, err)
178	}
179	
180	// samplePackageFS returns an in-memory fs.FS rooted at the package layout that
181	// the remote tarball produces, so both entrypoints exercise the same manifest
182	// and mappings.
183	func samplePackageFS() fstest.MapFS {
184		return fstest.MapFS{
185			"package.yaml": &fstest.MapFile{Data: []byte(`name: sample-plugin
186	version: 1.0.0
187	description: Sample package
188	type: plugin
189	source: https://example.com/sample-plugin
190	api_version: "1"
191	install:
192	  root:
193	    source: .
194	    target: .ddx/plugins/sample-plugin
195	  skills:
196	    - source: skills/
197	      target: .agents/skills/
198	    - source: skills/
199	      target: .claude/skills/
200	`)},
201			"skills/ddx/SKILL.md": &fstest.MapFile{Data: []byte("---\nname: ddx\ndescription: sample\n---\n# ddx\n")},
202		}
203	}
204	
205	// TestInstallPackageFromFSHonorsManifestMappings proves that an embedded
206	// fs.FS rooted at a package directory reads package.yaml and applies both
207	// the root mapping and the declared skill mappings.
208	func TestInstallPackageFromFSHonorsManifestMappings(t *testing.T) {
209		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
210		pkg := sampleInstallPackage("https://example.com/sample-plugin")
211	
212		entry, err := InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, samplePackageFS(), projectRoot)
213		require.NoError(t, err)
214	
215		// Root mapping: package.yaml from the embedded FS lands under .ddx/plugins/<name>/.
216		rootManifest := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "plugins", "sample-plugin", "package.yaml")
217		_, statErr := os.Stat(rootManifest)
218		require.NoError(t, statErr, "package.yaml should be installed via root mapping")
219	
220		// Skill mappings: each declared target gets the skill directory under it.
221		for _, rel := range []string{
222			filepath.Join(".agents", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
223			filepath.Join(".claude", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
224		} {
225			path := filepath.Join(projectRoot, rel)
226			data, readErr := os.ReadFile(path)
227			require.NoError(t, readErr, "skill file should exist via manifest skill mapping: %s", rel)
228			assert.Contains(t, string(data), "ddx")
229			assert.Contains(t, entry.Files, rel)
230		}
231	}
232	
233	// TestInstallPackageFromFSInstallsDefaultDDxPackageOffline proves the embedded
234	// default ddx package can install .ddx/plugins/ddx, .agents/skills/ddx, and
235	// .claude/skills/ddx without invoking any network download code.
236	func TestInstallPackageFromFSInstallsDefaultDDxPackageOffline(t *testing.T) {
237		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
238	
239		// Block network: any HTTP attempt should fail this test loudly.
240		origTransport := http.DefaultTransport
241		http.DefaultTransport = &offlineTransport{t: t}
242		t.Cleanup(func() { http.DefaultTransport = origTransport })
243	
244		pkg := &Package{
245			Name:    "ddx",
246			Version: "0.0.0",
247			Type:    PackageTypePlugin,
248			Source:  "https://example.com/ddx",
249		}
250	
251		entry, err := InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, defaultplugin.FS(), projectRoot)
252		require.NoError(t, err)
253	
254		for _, rel := range []string{
255			filepath.Join(".ddx", "plugins", "ddx", "package.yaml"),
256			filepath.Join(".agents", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
257			filepath.Join(".claude", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
258		} {
259			path := filepath.Join(projectRoot, rel)
260			_, statErr := os.Stat(path)
261			require.NoError(t, statErr, "expected embedded default package to install %s", rel)
262		}
263	
264		// The recorded file list must include the skill targets so the install
265		// state can be uninstalled/reverified later.
266		for _, rel := range []string{
267			filepath.Join(".agents", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
268			filepath.Join(".claude", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
269		} {
270			assert.Contains(t, entry.Files, rel)
271		}
272	}
273	
274	// TestInstallPackageFromRemoteUsesSharedCore proves the remote entrypoint
275	// and embedded-FS entrypoint share the same skill mapping behavior for a
276	// fixture package — same recorded files, same on-disk layout.
277	func TestInstallPackageFromRemoteUsesSharedCore(t *testing.T) {
278		remoteRoot := t.TempDir()
279		fsRoot := t.TempDir()
280	
281		server := newPackageArchiveServer(t, samplePackageTarball(t))
282		defer server.Close()
283	
284		remoteEntry, err := InstallPackageFromRemote(sampleInstallPackage(server.URL), remoteRoot)
285		require.NoError(t, err)
286	
287		fsEntry, err := InstallPackageFromFS(sampleInstallPackage(server.URL), samplePackageFS(), fsRoot)
288		require.NoError(t, err)
289	
290		// Both entrypoints record the same project-relative install paths.
291		remoteFiles := append([]string(nil), remoteEntry.Files...)
292		fsFiles := append([]string(nil), fsEntry.Files...)
293		sort.Strings(remoteFiles)
294		sort.Strings(fsFiles)
295		assert.Equal(t, remoteFiles, fsFiles, "remote and FS entrypoints should record identical install file lists")
296	
297		// And the same skill files actually exist under each project root.
298		for _, rel := range []string{
299			filepath.Join(".agents", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
300			filepath.Join(".claude", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
301		} {
302			_, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(remoteRoot, rel))
303			require.NoError(t, err, "remote install missing %s", rel)
304			_, err = os.Stat(filepath.Join(fsRoot, rel))
305			require.NoError(t, err, "fs install missing %s", rel)
306		}
307	}
308	
309	// offlineTransport fails any HTTP attempt so tests can prove an install path
310	// did not reach the network.
311	type offlineTransport struct{ t *testing.T }
312	
313	func (o *offlineTransport) RoundTrip(req *http.Request) (*http.Response, error) {
314		o.t.Helper()
315		o.t.Fatalf("unexpected HTTP request during offline install: %s %s", req.Method, req.URL.String())
316		return nil, nil
317	}
318

=== RUN   TestInstallPackageFromFSHonorsManifestMappings
--- PASS: TestInstallPackageFromFSHonorsManifestMappings (0.00s)
=== RUN   TestInstallPackageFromFSInstallsDefaultDDxPackageOffline
--- PASS: TestInstallPackageFromFSInstallsDefaultDDxPackageOffline (0.00s)
